stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking activity around a significant open-source LLM.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags such as 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The content points to the continued relevance and community interest in Meta's Llama family of models.

stories.title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: NASA has selected Relativity Space, led by Eric Schmidt, for a 2028 mission to Mars.
Relativity Space will handle the entire mission scope, including the spacecraft, rocket, and cruise operations.
The mission involves launching NASA's Aeolus payload to Mars under a new public-private partnership.

stories.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft has discovered a new, lightweight backdoor malware named Crypto Clipper.
The malware is capable of stealing cryptocurrency from infected systems.
It spreads via USB drives and utilizes the Tor network for communication.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: The research identifies that fine-tuning language models on insecure code causes 'emergent misalignment' related to poorly understood internal structures.
The study proposes and validates an 'actionable activation-space direction' that can detect this misalignment across multiple model architectures.
This direction achieved a high separation rate (99.6%) between aligned and misaligned activations in four different instruction-tuned model families.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in higher quality model outputs.
The updated engine also delivers faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, enhancing local AI capabilities on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It connects users to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: The Atlantic has made public four datasets of music used to train AI models.
Two of the identified music datasets are massive, containing 12 million and 9 million tracks.
This development highlights the transparency and scale of data fueling current AI music training.

stories.title: FDA advisors unanimously vote to approve Moderna's mRNA after agency drama
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: The FDA advisors unanimously approved Moderna's mRNA vaccine.
The approval followed a period of 'agency drama,' including a refusal by a Trump official to review the vaccine in February.
The story highlights the regulatory and political hurdles in the vaccine approval process.

stories.title: As China looms, Taiwan makes more drones for defense and the US military
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Taiwan is increasing its drone production for national defense.
This defense spending is also expected to stimulate business growth internationally.
The context of escalating tensions with China is driving this military hardware buildup.

stories.title: Android verification is coming: Google confirms timeline and supported app stores
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is implementing a new Android verification system service this month.
Significant changes related to this verification are scheduled to begin in September.
The update confirms timelines and details regarding supported app stores for the new system.

stories.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
stories.author: Nityanand Mathur
bullet_points: FlowEdit addresses a key limitation in state-of-the-art flow-matching TTS: the inability to adapt to new pronunciations post-deployment.
It introduces a lifelong adaptation framework that learns pronunciation corrections via latent conditioning edits rather than full model retraining.
The method optimizes token-level perturbations in the text embedding space when corrective feedback is provided, enabling dynamic pronunciation fixes.

stories.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
stories.author: Jana Diesner
bullet_points: Introduces StylisticBias, a new controlled benchmark for evaluating attribute-level social bias in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap in MLLM research by separating appearance-based effects from genuine identity differences when models judge people.
Highlights the growing societal risk associated with poorly understood visual cues influencing consequential MLLM deployments.

stories.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
stories.author: Hamed Zamani
bullet_points: The paper addresses the high cost and scarcity of explicit human feedback used in current LLM alignment methods.
It proposes leveraging implicit user feedback (like mouse movements or gaze) to align Large Language Models.
This approach aims to incorporate valuable behavioral data, similar to how major internet platforms operate, into LLM training.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The content suggests tracking model performance and usage via standard ML tooling (TensorBoard, safetensors).

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware platforms.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine specifically for improved functionality on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation functionality.
The feature is currently experimental and is available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: The UK will scan asylum-seekers’ faces for age checks—despite knowing the tech is flawed
stories.author: Matt Burgess, Maddy Varner, May Bulman, Gabriel Geiger, WIRED.com
bullet_points: The UK government plans to use facial scanning technology for age verification of asylum seekers.
Independent tests have demonstrated significant risks associated with the accuracy of this age-verification technology.
The implementation proceeds despite known flaws in the technology, raising ethical and accuracy concerns.

stories.title: NASA asks Northrop Grumman to stop working on lunar HALO module
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA has directed Northrop Grumman to cease work on the lunar HALO module.
The cancellation involves a significant program component related to lunar exploration.
Affected employees are being reassigned to other ongoing NASA opportunities and programs.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and another component (implied).

stories.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Sudarshan Kamath
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of understanding regarding how specific words influence the acoustic output in style-captioned Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
A novel method, cross-attention attribution, is proposed and adapted from the DAAM framework specifically for speech diffusion models.
The technique generates per-token heatmaps across multiple layers to improve the diagnosis of failure modes and enhance controllability in expressive TTS.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to give policy-adherent agents a structured state representation.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where task states (facts, constraints, conditions) are implicitly reconstructed from the prompt.
Focuses on improving reliability in complex domains like customer service by explicitly tracking task state across multi-turn interactions and tool calls.

stories.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
stories.author: Fengxiang He
bullet_points: DeepSWIP addresses the limitation of associational inference in neurosymbolic systems like DeepProbLog by introducing a single-world counterfactual semantics.
The method integrates neural perception with probabilistic logic by using neural materialization to convert fixed-context neural predicates into standard ProbLog choices.
It applies Single World Intervention Programs (SWIPs) to enable causal reasoning and compute counterfactuals within the system.

stories.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
stories.author: Georgia Channing
bullet_points: SARLO-80 introduces a new, large-scale dataset designed to bridge the gap between optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data.
The dataset addresses limitations in existing SAR-optical resources by preserving complex-valued SAR measurements and native acquisition geometry.
This development is crucial for advancing physically grounded multimodal foundation models in remote sensing and Earth observation.

stories.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
stories.author: Dmitrii Babaev
bullet_points: The paper introduces Multi-LCB, an extension of LiveCodeBench (LCB) to support multiple programming languages.
LCB is a recognized benchmark for evaluating LLMs on code generation, known for its contamination-aware evaluation.
The research addresses a limitation of LCB by enabling LLMs to be tested for generalization across diverse languages beyond Python.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The research investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM that operates significantly in a continuous latent space.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and another component (implied).

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Introduces 'privacy via predictability' as a new, fine-grained privacy framework.
Addresses the limitations of traditional Differential Privacy (DP) by moving beyond worst-case guarantees.
Explicitly incorporates attacker knowledge, compromised data portions, and query families to measure leakage.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in current LLM serving by focusing on low-latency, small-batch, on-device physical-AI serving, unlike high-throughput systems.
Proposes 'Execution-State Capsules' to manage the full execution state, not just the standard KV cache, which is crucial for interactive agents.
Targets use cases like interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies that require frequent branching and rapid state restoration.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: The paper addresses the need for LLM-based coding agents to possess high-level operational knowledge about a codebase (e.g., subsystem locations, test procedures).
It investigates the effectiveness of using documentation like 'AGENTS.md' files to provide this necessary context to coding agents.
The research introduces a 'Probe-and-Refine Tuning' method to improve agent performance by better integrating repository guidance.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a new framework for building policy-adherent tool-calling agents.
Addresses a key limitation in current agents: the lack of a separate, structured representation for task state across conversational turns.
Proposes that task states (facts, constraints, conditions) should be explicitly maintained rather than being reconstructed solely from the prompt history.

stories.title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
stories.author: Thomas Brox
bullet_points: Introduces RefRad2D, a large-scale, bilingual (German/English) dataset of 1.2M CT/MR image-text pairs for radiology.
Develops RefRad2D to train Spatially Grounded Vision-Language Models (VLMs) without requiring manual spatial annotations.
The resulting model, RadGrounder, performs multiple tasks including report generation and visual question answering.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the overlap between hate speech and misinformation, a gap in current NLP research.
Proposes a new dataset ('CATCH-ME') to provide high-quality, contextually annotated examples for training LLMs.
Aims to improve LLM performance in generating effective, non-repetitive counterspeech against harmful online content.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: The paper focuses on token-operations-oriented inference optimization for large language models (LLMs).
It introduces a novel four-layer technical architecture: Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, Compute-Model Fusion, and Compute-Network-Model Fusion.
The research aims to improve the scalability, cost-efficiency, and stability of large model services.

stories.title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
stories.author: Celestine Achi
bullet_points: Introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework (MIF) for analyzing Nigerian public discourse.
MIF is a nine-dimension schema designed to differentiate true communicative intent from surface-level sentiment.
The research argues that AI failure in Nigerian discourse stems from context failure, not just translation issues, challenging existing three-way sentiment benchmarks.

stories.title: Anthropic to Require ID Verification for Certain Capabilities Starting July 8
stories.author: bathory
bullet_points: Anthropic is implementing ID verification requirements for specific AI capabilities.
The enforcement of these new verification policies is scheduled to begin on July 8th.
This move suggests increasing regulatory or safety compliance measures within the advanced AI sector.

stories.title: Apple Internals: Swift in the Kernel
stories.author: blog.calif.io via heavyrain266
bullet_points: The article discusses Apple's internal use of the Swift programming language within the kernel.
This suggests a significant, though internal, shift in Apple's low-level system development practices.
The topic is highly relevant to systems programming and operating system architecture.

stories.title: How Graphify Stopped My Team from Burning Thousands of Tokens Per Query
stories.author: vikrantnegi
bullet_points: The article discusses using 'Graphify' to manage large codebases for AI queries.
The core problem addressed is excessive token usage when feeding large numbers of files (12 per question) to AI tools like Cursor.
The solution involves transforming the React Native codebase into a knowledge graph, allowing for scoped subgraph querying instead of full context feeding.

stories.title: Your AI agent has sudo. I built a tool to take it away.
stories.author: hasanmehmood
bullet_points: The article addresses a critical security vulnerability where an AI agent was granted excessive permissions (sudo access).
The author developed a tool specifically designed to revoke or restrict the dangerous privileges of AI agents.
It highlights the growing real-world security concerns surrounding the integration of AI agents into local machine environments.
